checks.actual: All 7 columns present with correct names
checks.notes: CSV header matches expected DESeq2 output structure
Both Nextflow processes exited with code 0
Patch-level difference only; same minor version, fully compatible
Minor version difference (4.4.0 vs 4.3.0). Within Bioconductor RELEASE_3_18 container which is the recommended environment.
Major version difference (4.0.3 vs 3.5.0). Comes from bioconductor_docker:RELEASE_3_18 container. Plotting output is functionally correct.
Direction matches: Gene_A is significantly upregulated
Direction matches: Gene_B is significantly downregulated
Exactly 2 genes with padj < 0.05, matching paper claim
Deviation of +0.42 (16.8% relative). Direction and significance consistent. Likely due to synthetic/idealized paper values vs actual DESeq2 computation on count data.
Deviation of -0.20 (11.1% relative). Direction and significance consistent. Same likely cause as R4.

Orders of magnitude difference. Paper value appears idealized/synthetic. Actual count data (Control mean ~111 vs Treatment mean ~858) produces extremely significant p-value. Significance call (padj < 0.05) is consistent.
Orders of magnitude difference. Same likely cause as R6. Significance call is consistent.
All 8 non-DE genes correctly identified as not significant
Both PNG and PDF formats generated successfully
Confirmed via visual inspection of generated volcano plot. Gene_A clearly in upper-right with red color indicating significance.
Confirmed via visual inspection. Gene_B clearly in upper-left with red color indicating significance.
Confirmed via visual inspection. All non-significant genes form a tight cluster near the origin.

checks.expected: Gene_A up, Gene_B down, others not DE
checks.actual: Gene_A significantly upregulated (padj=4.37e-119), Gene_B significantly downregulated (padj=5.57e-42), Gene_C–J not significant
checks.notes: Core biological conclusions fully reproduced. The qualitative findings are identical to the paper despite numerical magnitude differences in log2FC and padj values.

deviations.deviation: Gene_A log2FC = 2.92 vs paper claim of 2.5
deviations.likely_cause: Paper reports idealized/synthetic values; actual DESeq2 computation on count data produces slightly different estimate

deviations.deviation: Gene_B log2FC = -2.00 vs paper claim of -1.8
Gene_A padj = 4.37e-119 vs paper claim of 0.0008
deviations.likely_cause: Paper uses idealized p-values; actual count data with large fold change and low variance produces extremely small p-value

deviations.deviation: Gene_B padj = 5.57e-42 vs paper claim of 0.004
R version 4.4.0 vs paper specification of 4.3.0
deviations.likely_cause: Bioconductor RELEASE_3_18 Docker container ships R 4.4.0

deviations.deviation: ggplot2 version 4.0.3 vs paper specification of 3.5.0
deviations.likely_cause: Bioconductor RELEASE_3_18 Docker container ships ggplot2 4.0.3
